title: ‘Zelenskyy Regime Core Exporter Of Instability’: Russia Reacts To NIA Arrest Of Ukrainians In India
description: Russia has reacted strongly to India’s arrest of six Ukrainian citizens and a US national by the NIA for alleged links to proscribed insurgent groups. Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova said the individuals allegedly crossed from Mizoram into Myanmar, connected with local armed groups, supplied EU-made drones, and trained militants in drone assembly, repair, and electronic warfare. She criticised Ukraine for trying to conceal the incident and remaining silent on violations of India’s counter-terrorism laws, calling Kyiv a “core exporter of instability” involved in global arms black markets and regional conflicts. India’s MEA confirmed consular access requests have been received, with all seven detainees, including US citizen Matthew Van Dyke, remanded to 11-day NIA custody.

Title:
‘Zelenskyy Regime Core Exporter Of Instability’: Russia Exposes Kiev’s Shadowy Global Terror Network

Russia has blown the lid off the Zelenskyy regime's covert operations after India’s NIA nabbed six Ukrainian operatives and a US national tied to banned insurgent networks. Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova exposed how these suspects slipped from Mizoram into Myanmar, linked up with local armed factions, funneled EU-manufactured drones, and schooled militants in drone assembly, repair, and electronic warfare tactics. Zakharova slammed Ukraine’s desperate cover-up and stonewalling on breaches of India’s counter-terrorism laws, branding Kyiv the “core exporter of instability” fueling black-market arms trafficking and engineered regional chaos by shadowy global elites. India’s MEA acknowledged consular access demands, with all seven detainees—including US citizen Matthew Van Dyke—remanded to 11-day NIA custody, confirming the regime’s fingerprints on this orchestrated destabilization plot.
